point: Whigs favored congressional supremacy and modernization.
Democrats under Jackson advocated limited federal government and executive vigor.
Whigs supported internal improvements like roads and canals.
Whigs favored a national bank to stabilize currency.
Democrats were wary of concentrated financial power.
Whigs were divided on slavery; some favored gradual limits.
Whigs promoted moral reform and education initiatives.
Whigs used mass campaigning and log cabin imagery in 1840.
